rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 4, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JOHNA PECOT, THOMAS ARATA, RICH OWYANG, STEPHEN TILTON, JOSEPH LEAKE, OSCAR TAYLOR'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Granted. Defendants shall pay Plaintiff reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $12,840 pursuant to CCP sec. 425.16(c)(1). The Court has already made a finding that the Anti-SLAPP motion filed by Defendants was frivolous, as reflected in the January 12, 2015 Order. rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473 (D) To Correct Clerical Error In Order After Demurrer And Slapp Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 4, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT DAVID WONG'S Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473 (D) To Correct Clerical Error In Order After Demurrer And Slapp Hearing. Denied. The January 12, 2015 Order accurately reflects the Court's ruling on the Anti-SLAPP motion. Although Plaintiff stipulated to removing allegations pertaining to the dismissed Defendants on the record at the hearing, Defendants did not prevail on their Anti-SLAPP motion. As stated in the Order, an Anti-SLAPP motion can only be used to challenge causes of action and not specific factual allegations. A regular motion to strike would have been the proper procedural mechanism for achieving the results sought from the Anti-SLAPP motion. Further, Defendants engaged in no meaningful analysis as to whether the first prong of the statute was met. Thus, the Order is accurate, including the statement that the Anti-SLAPP motion was frivolous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER SEIDEL VS. RICHARD D NELSON ESQ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Bancroft & Mcalister, Llps Further Response To Special Interrogatory No. 33 And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 4, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER SEIDEL'S Motion To Compel Defendant Bancroft & Mcalister, LLP's Further Response To Special Interrogatory No. 33 And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel further response to Special Interrogatory 33 is granted. The defendants properly exercised their option to produce documents in lieu of a written response pursuant to CCP 2030.230. However defendants failed to specify which writings of the 2,390 pages produced are responsive to the special interrogatory. Defendants shall specify each document which supports their contention by Bates stamp number and/or date of transcript and page number within ten court days from the date of this Order. Specification by defendants' counsel of evidentiary documents is not protected attorney work-product. See Cioto vs. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal. 4th 480. Both parties' request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IRINA SIGNAEVICH et al VS. THOMAS JEONG GEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendant Edwin M. Gees Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Default Under C.C.P. 473.5
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 4, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT EDWIN GEE'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendant Edwin M. Gees Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Default Under C.C.P. 473.5. Denied. "A motion for reconsideration must be based on new or different facts, circumstances, or law, and facts of which the party seeking reconsideration was aware at the time of the original ruling are not new or different." (In re Marriage of Herr (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1463, 1468.) Additionally, "a party must provide a satisfactory explanation for failing to offer the evidence in the first instance." (Id.) Defendant does not set forth new or different facts that were not available to him at the time of the initial hearing. Further, Defendant does not provide a satisfactory explanation as to why this evidence was not provided in the first instance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: BRACAMONTES & VLASAK, P.C. VS. ELI SADDLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 4, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT ELI SADDLER, MISTY MCKINNEY'S Special Motion To Strike. The motion is denied. Plaintiff's complaint seeks to recover because defendants breached the retainer agreement and falsely represented that they would pay a 35% contingency fee. None of plaintiff's claims are based on protected activity and CCP 425.16 does not apply. The motion is frivolous. Plaintiff is entitled to $3,737.50 in fees and costs pursuant to CCP 425.16(c)(1). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
